input.background: Recall that event A is cat ownership and event B is dog ownership. We have established Pr(B | A)= 0.4286, Pr(Ā)=1-Pr(A)=1-0.35=0.65, and Pr(B and Ā)=0.15.
input.question: By conditional probability, Pr(B | Ā)=Pr(B and Ā)/Pr(Ā)=0.15/0.65=0.2308.

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

Pr(A).description: The probability of event A, which represents cat ownership.

Pr(Ā).description: The probability of the complement of event A, which represents not owning a cat.

Pr(B | A).description: The conditional probability of event B (dog ownership) given event A (cat ownership).

Pr(B and Ā).description: The joint probability of event B (dog ownership) and the complement of event A (not owning a cat).

Pr(B | Ā).description: The conditional probability of event B (dog ownership) given the complement of event A (not owning a cat).

meta_info.caption: Total probability rule (Pet ownership).
meta_info.section: 5.5  MORE RULES AND PROPERTIES OF PROBABILITY
